verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the beta subunit of mitochondrial ATP synthase (Complex V), canonically located in the mitochondrial inner membrane facing the matrix. However, there is well-documented evidence that ATP synthase subunits, including the beta subunit (also called beta-F1-ATPase or ATP synthase beta), are ectopically displayed on the plasma membrane outer leaflet of tumor cells and endothelial cells. Surface-expressed ATP synthase beta has been detected by surface biotinylation and flow cytometry on intact cancer cells and endothelial cells, where it serves as a receptor for angiostatin, HDL particles, and other ligands. Anti-ATP synthase beta antibodies have been shown to bind live, non-permeabilized tumor cells. This is a documented, gene-specific cell-state-induced (oncogenic/endothelial activation) surface pool qualifying as contextual/cell_state_induced. The dominant localization remains mitochondrial inner membrane, but the outer-leaflet ecto-pool in tumor and endothelial contexts is well-supported experimentally.
predicted_key_uncertainty: The mechanism of PM translocation for this mitochondrial protein lacks a TM anchor for the outer leaflet; it may be peripherally associated or held by a partner, and the robustness of the surface pool across tumor types is variable.
